title: Distributed Systems Theory
learningObjectives: Explain **CAP** and **PACELC** with concrete system behaviors, not slogans
Describe **quorum** reasoning and **leader** election failure modes at a high level
Connect **chaos** experiments to **blast** radius controls and learning goals

sections.title: Why Distributed Systems Theory matters
sections.content:
**Distributed systems** interviews test whether you can **name** trade-offs (**CAP**, **latency vs consistency**) and connect them to **operational** patterns—**idempotency**, **retries**, **versioning**, and **observability**.

Messaging days built intuition; this day sharpens **theory** vocabulary for **staff** loops.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Distributed Systems Theory
sections.content:
### Day 67 — **Distributed** **systems** **theory**

### 1. CAP / PACELC
Partition behavior vs steady-state latency trade-offs.

### 2. Consistency models
**Linearizability**, **serializability**, **eventual**.

### 3. Concurrency control
**Optimistic** vs **pessimistic**; **version** fields.

### 4. Time and ordering
**Vector** clocks, **happens-before**, **quorum** ideas.

### 5. Reliability patterns
**Backpressure**, **bulkheads**, **timeouts**.

### 6. Story
**Optimistic** lock without **UX** retry causes **support** spikes; add **version** conflict flows.

sections.title: Basic — CAP one-liner
sections.output:
CAP: during partition, balance consistency vs availability
PACELC: latency vs consistency even without partition

sections.title: Intermediate — Assignment CAP print
Advanced — Version increment toy
sections.description: Simulate OCC conflict on stale version.

sections.title: Quorum read/write overlap
sections.description: R + W > N allows tunable staleness vs durability.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 67 — Quorum intuition
note right: Example: N=3, R=2, W=2
participant Writer
participant Reader
database Replicas
Writer -> Replicas : write quorum
Reader -> Replicas : read quorum
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Distributed systems theory — CAP (Day 67 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly**: Consistency vs Availability (Partition tolerance assumed)

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: State **CAP** cleanly.
conceptual.answer: During a **network partition**, you cannot have both **strong linearizable** availability for all operations *and* **full** consistency without compromise—systems pick **CP** or **AP** flavors in practice. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **distributed systems theory**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. During a **network partition**, you cannot have both **strong linearizable** availability for all op Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. During a **network partition**, you cannot have both **strong linearizable** availability Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

conceptual.question: **PACELC** extension?
conceptual.answer: If **partition** (P) then choose **A** vs **C**; **else** (E) choose **latency (L)** vs **consistency (C)** for normal operation. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

conceptual.question: Define **linearizability**.
conceptual.answer: Operations appear to occur atomically at **one** point on a **global** timeline matching real-time order observable by concurrent clients. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. conceptual.question: **Eventual consistency** meaning?
conceptual.answer: If updates stop, replicas **converge** to same state given enough time—does not specify **when**. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. wrongAnswers: **Eventual** consistency means **immediate** wrong reads ok forever — **Correction:** **Converges** when quiescent.
**Vector** clocks make conflicts disappear — **Correction:** They **detect**; **resolution** still needed.
**Chaos** in prod Friday — **Correction:** **Blast** radius control matters.
**Quorum** math always used in SQL DB — **Correction:** Single-node **primary** common.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Term | Recall |
|---|---|
| CAP | Partition trade-offs |
| PACELC | Latency vs consistency |
| Linearizable | Real-time global order |
| Serializable | Transaction interleaving |
| Eventual | Converge when quiet |
| OCC | Version conflicts |
| Vector clock | Causality detection |
| Idempotency key | Safe retries |
| Quorum | R+W>N style |
| Backpressure | Slow upstream signal |
